Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jan-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Phillip Lozano

RULING:
DEFENDANT PATRICIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Patricia Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Chandler Lumber delivered potentially asbestos-containing products to his jobsites. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiff's claim. Further, evidence submitted by defendant - the deposition testimony of plaintiff and Marc Myers - creates a triable issue of material fact whether Chandler Lumber delivered non-lumber products to plaintiff's jobsites, which precludes summary judgment. This was the sole issue properly raised by defendant's moving papers. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Myers' deposition testimony are overruled as to objection no. 1 and sustained as to objection nos. 2 and 3 as to any statements regarding Chandler Lumber prior to 1970. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)
